forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY0HNVTKN7ZJ85H8ZJN2WJD5
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a880
+title: Stream actual review findings + verdicts over MCP notifications/message as they resolve
+---
+## What
+
+Stream the review engine's actual results over MCP as they are produced ‚Äî not just progress ticks ‚Äî so an MCP client (e.g. the main agent driving a review) can start resolving findings in parallel while the run is still going. Builds on the ^jn2wjd5 event channel/bridge (depends on it).
+
+Ground truth about finality (verified in code): fan-out validator tasks run independently (`collect_fan_out`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`), and verification is per-finding and independent ‚Äî `verify_findings` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs`) runs each candidate through the deterministic `run_guard` and then its own adversarial verify task, pipelined on the same pool; `synthesize` only exact-dedups, it never revises verdicts. So each finding's data is final at two well-defined moments: its raw content when its validator task completes, and its confirmed/refuted verdict when its verify task resolves. Stream both.
+
+Transport: `notifications/progress` carries only progress/total/message ‚Äî wrong channel for content. Use MCP logging notifications (`notifications/message`), which carry arbitrary structured `data`; the peer path already exists (`send_mcp_log` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs` / `peer.notify_logging_message`). Full payloads always ‚Äî never truncate a finding.
+
+Implementation shape:
+
+1. **Engine events** ‚Äî extend `ReviewProgressEvent` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`, added by ^jn2wjd5) with two content-carrying variants:
+   - `Findings { validator: String, findings: Vec<Finding> }` ‚Äî emitted in `collect_fan_out` at each validator task's resolution with the task's parsed, validator-tagged findings (empty vec = that validator came back clean; failed task = no Findings event, the existing PairDone/failure accounting covers it).
+   - `Verdict { finding: Finding, confirmed: bool, reason: String }` ‚Äî emitted as each candidate's verdict resolves: guard refutations in/after `run_guard`, agent verdicts in `collect_verify`/`verify_findings` (`verify.rs`). Requires threading the existing optional progress sender from `run_review_over_agent` (`drive.rs`) into the verify stage (it already reaches the fleet stage from ^jn2wjd5).
+2. **MCP bridge** ‚Äî in `spawn_review_progress_bridge` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs`, from ^jn2wjd5), map the two new variants to `LoggingMessageNotificationParam` with structured `data` (e.g. `{"kind": "review.findings", "validator": ‚Ä¶, "findings": [full Finding JSON‚Ä¶]}` / `{"kind": "review.verdict", "finding": ‚Ä¶, "confirmed": ‚Ä¶, "reason": ‚Ä¶}`, logger `"review"`, level Info) sent via the peer; keep progress-tick variants flowing to `notifications/progress` as today. When only an in-process `progress_sink` exists (no peer), skip logging notifications ‚Äî the sink contract is progress params.
+3. Document the streamed message shapes in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/description.md` ‚Äî note events are per-validator granular while the final report exact-dedups across validators (dedup, never retraction).
+
+Subtasks:
+- [ ] `Findings` + `Verdict` variants on `ReviewProgressEvent`; emit from `collect_fan_out`, guard refutations, and `collect_verify`
+- [ ] Thread the progress sender into `verify_findings` (drive.rs ‚Üí verify.rs)
+- [ ] Bridge maps content variants ‚Üí `notifications/message` with full structured payloads (progress ticks unchanged)
+- [ ] Document message shapes in description.md
+- [ ] Tests (below)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] During a review with a live peer, each validator task's completion produces one `notifications/message` carrying every parsed finding for that validator, before the final tool result arrives
+- [ ] Each finding's verdict (confirmed or refuted, with reason ‚Äî both guard- and agent-decided) produces its own `notifications/message` as it resolves
+- [ ] Streamed finding payloads are complete Finding JSON (file, rule, validator, claim/description fields) ‚Äî no truncation, no summaries
+- [ ] The final `ReviewReport` is unchanged, and the union of streamed confirmed verdicts matches the report's findings modulo synthesize's exact dedup
+- [ ] With no peer (in-process sink only, or neither), zero logging notifications are emitted and existing behavior is unchanged
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Fleet/verify unit tests (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet/tests.rs`, `verify.rs` test mod, scripted agents): assert `Findings` events per completed validator task (including a clean/empty one) and `Verdict` events for a guard-refuted, an agent-confirmed, and an agent-refuted candidate
+- [ ] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/review_progress_notifications_test.rs` (or a sibling e2e): client also captures `notifications/message`; assert finding + verdict messages arrive with full structured data during the run and that confirmed streamed findings match the final report
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators review` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(review)'` ‚Äî green, scripted agents only
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-## What
-
-Stream the review engine's actual results over MCP as they are produced ‚Äî not just progress ticks ‚Äî so an MCP client (e.g. the main agent driving a review) can start resolving findings in parallel while the run is still going. Builds on the ^jn2wjd5 event channel/bridge (depends on it).
-
-Ground truth about finality (verified in code): fan-out validator tasks run independently (`collect_fan_out`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`), and verification is per-finding and independent ‚Äî `verify_findings` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs`) runs each candidate through the deterministic `run_guard` and then its own adversarial verify task, pipelined on the same pool; `synthesize` only exact-dedups, it never revises verdicts. So each finding's data is final at two well-defined moments: its raw content when its validator task completes, and its confirmed/refuted verdict when its verify task resolves. Stream both.
-
-Transport: `notifications/progress` carries only progress/total/message ‚Äî wrong channel for content. Use MCP logging notifications (`notifications/message`), which carry arbitrary structured `data`; the peer path already exists (`send_mcp_log` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tool_registry.rs` / `peer.notify_logging_message`). Full payloads always ‚Äî never truncate a finding.
-
-Implementation shape:
-
-1. **Engine events** ‚Äî extend `ReviewProgressEvent` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs`, added by ^jn2wjd5) with two content-carrying variants:
-   - `Findings { validator: String, findings: Vec<Finding> }` ‚Äî emitted in `collect_fan_out` at each validator task's resolution with the task's parsed, validator-tagged findings (empty vec = that validator came back clean; failed task = no Findings event, the existing PairDone/failure accounting covers it).
-   - `Verdict { finding: Finding, confirmed: bool, reason: String }` ‚Äî emitted as each candidate's verdict resolves: guard refutations in/after `run_guard`, agent verdicts in `collect_verify`/`verify_findings` (`verify.rs`). Requires threading the existing optional progress sender from `run_review_over_agent` (`drive.rs`) into the verify stage (it already reaches the fleet stage from ^jn2wjd5).
-2. **MCP bridge** ‚Äî in `spawn_review_progress_bridge` (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs`, from ^jn2wjd5), map the two new variants to `LoggingMessageNotificationParam` with structured `data` (e.g. `{"kind": "review.findings", "validator": ‚Ä¶, "findings": [full Finding JSON‚Ä¶]}` / `{"kind": "review.verdict", "finding": ‚Ä¶, "confirmed": ‚Ä¶, "reason": ‚Ä¶}`, logger `"review"`, level Info) sent via the peer; keep progress-tick variants flowing to `notifications/progress` as today. When only an in-process `progress_sink` exists (no peer), skip logging notifications ‚Äî the sink contract is progress params.
-3. Document the streamed message shapes in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/description.md` ‚Äî note events are per-validator granular while the final report exact-dedups across validators (dedup, never retraction).
-
-Subtasks:
-- [ ] `Findings` + `Verdict` variants on `ReviewProgressEvent`; emit from `collect_fan_out`, guard refutations, and `collect_verify`
-- [ ] Thread the progress sender into `verify_findings` (drive.rs ‚Üí verify.rs)
-- [ ] Bridge maps content variants ‚Üí `notifications/message` with full structured payloads (progress ticks unchanged)
-- [ ] Document message shapes in description.md
-- [ ] Tests (below)
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] During a review with a live peer, each validator task's completion produces one `notifications/message` carrying every parsed finding for that validator, before the final tool result arrives
-- [ ] Each finding's verdict (confirmed or refuted, with reason ‚Äî both guard- and agent-decided) produces its own `notifications/message` as it resolves
-- [ ] Streamed finding payloads are complete Finding JSON (file, rule, validator, claim/description fields) ‚Äî no truncation, no summaries
-- [ ] The final `ReviewReport` is unchanged, and the union of streamed confirmed verdicts matches the report's findings modulo synthesize's exact dedup
-- [ ] With no peer (in-process sink only, or neither), zero logging notifications are emitted and existing behavior is unchanged
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Fleet/verify unit tests (`crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet/tests.rs`, `verify.rs` test mod, scripted agents): assert `Findings` events per completed validator task (including a clean/empty one) and `Verdict` events for a guard-refuted, an agent-confirmed, and an agent-refuted candidate
-- [ ] Extend `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/review_progress_notifications_test.rs` (or a sibling e2e): client also captures `notifications/message`; assert finding + verdict messages arrive with full structured data during the run and that confirmed streamed findings match the final report
-- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-validators review` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -E 'test(review)'` ‚Äî green, scripted agents only
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
